ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: By the dawn
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: By the dawn,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [by] ten nights
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the ten nights,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [by] the even [number] and the odd
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the even and the odd,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [by] the night when it passes,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the night when it passes!

ayahs.textArabic: هَلۡ فِي ذَٰلِكَ قَسَمٞ لِّذِي حِجۡرٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Hal fee thalika qasamun lithee hijrin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Is there [not] in [all] that an oath [sufficient] for one of perception?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Is all this ˹not˺ a sufficient oath for those who have sense?

ayahs.textArabic: أَلَمۡ تَرَ كَيۡفَ فَعَلَ رَبُّكَ بِعَادٍ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Alam tara kayfa faAAala rabbuka biAAadin
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Have you not considered how your Lord dealt with 'Aad -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Did you not see how your Lord dealt with ’Ȃd—

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [With] Iram - who had lofty pillars,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹the people˺ of Iram—with ˹their˺ great stature,

ayahs.textArabic: ٱلَّتِي لَمۡ يُخۡلَقۡ مِثۡلُهَا فِي ٱلۡبِلَٰدِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Allatee lam yukhlaq mithluha fee albiladi
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: The likes of whom had never been created in the land?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: unmatched in any other land;

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [with] Thamud, who carved out the rocks in the valley?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and Thamûd who carved ˹their homes into˺ the rocks in the ˹Stone˺ Valley;

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And [with] Pharaoh, owner of the stakes? -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and the Pharaoh of mighty structures?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [All of] whom oppressed within the lands
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They all transgressed throughout the land,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And increased therein the corruption.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: spreading much corruption there.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So your Lord poured upon them a scourge of punishment.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So your Lord unleashed on them a scourge of punishment.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Indeed, your Lord is in observation.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹For˺ your Lord is truly vigilant.

ayahs.textArabic: فَأَمَّا ٱلۡإِنسَٰنُ إِذَا مَا ٱبۡتَلَىٰهُ رَبُّهُۥ فَأَكۡرَمَهُۥ وَنَعَّمَهُۥ فَيَقُولُ رَبِّيٓ أَكۡرَمَنِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Faamma alinsanu itha ma ibtalahu rabbuhu faakramahu wanaAAAAamahu fayaqoolu rabbee akramani
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And as for man, when his Lord tries him and [thus] is generous to him and favors him, he says, "My Lord has honored me."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Now, whenever a human being is tested by their Lord through ˹His˺ generosity and blessings, they boast, “My Lord has ˹deservedly˺ honoured me!”

ayahs.textArabic: وَأَمَّآ إِذَا مَا ٱبۡتَلَىٰهُ فَقَدَرَ عَلَيۡهِ رِزۡقَهُۥ فَيَقُولُ رَبِّيٓ أَهَٰنَنِ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Waamma itha ma ibtalahu faqadara AAalayhi rizqahu fayaqoolu rabbee ahanani
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: But when He tries him and restricts his provision, he says, "My Lord has humiliated me."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: But when He tests them by limiting their provision, they protest, “My Lord has ˹undeservedly˺ humiliated me!”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! But you do not honor the orphan
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Absolutely not! In fact, you are not ˹even˺ gracious to the orphan,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And you do not encourage one another to feed the poor.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: nor do you urge one another to feed the poor.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And you consume inheritance, devouring [it] altogether,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: And you devour ˹others’˺ inheritance greedily,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And you love wealth with immense love.
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and love wealth fervently.

ayahs.textArabic: كـَلَّآۖ إِذَا دُكَّتِ ٱلۡأَرۡضُ دَكّٗا دَكّٗا
ayahs.textTransliteration: Kalla itha dukkati alardu dakkan dakkan
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: No! When the earth has been leveled - pounded and crushed -
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Enough! When the earth is entirely crushed over and over,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And your Lord has come and the angels, rank upon rank,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and your Lord comes ˹to judge˺ with angels, rank upon rank,

ayahs.textArabic: وَجِاْيٓءَ يَوۡمَئِذِۭ بِجَهَنَّمَۚ يَوۡمَئِذٖ يَتَذَكَّرُ ٱلۡإِنسَٰنُ وَأَنَّىٰ لَهُ ٱلذِّكۡرَىٰ
ayahs.textTransliteration: Wajeea yawmaithin bijahannama yawmaithin yatathakkaru alinsanu waanna lahu alththikra
ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And brought [within view], that Day, is Hell - that Day, man will remember, but what good to him will be the remembrance?
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and Hell is brought forth on that Day—this is when every ˹disbelieving˺ person will remember ˹their own sins˺. But what is the use of remembering then?

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: He will say, "Oh, I wish I had sent ahead [some good] for my life."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: They will cry, “I wish I had sent forth ˹something good˺ for my ˹true˺ life.”

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: So on that Day, none will punish [as severely] as His punishment,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: On that Day He will punish ˹them˺ severely, like no other,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And none will bind [as severely] as His binding [of the evildoers].
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and bind ˹them˺ tightly, like no other.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: [To the righteous it will be said], "O reassured soul,
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: ˹Allah will say to the righteous,˺ “O tranquil soul!

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: Return to your Lord, well-pleased and pleasing [to Him],
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: Return to your Lord, well pleased ˹with Him˺ and well pleasing ˹to Him˺.

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And enter among My [righteous] servants
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: So join My servants,

ayahs.textEnglishSaheeh: And enter My Paradise."
ayahs.textEnglishMustafa: and enter My Paradise.”
